title: Disruption of Chlorella vulgaris Microalga Cell Wall Protocol
author: Diogo Coelho

steps.title: Preparation of C
steps.instruction: Preparation of C. vulgaris suspension (20 mg/mL) resuspending the microalgae powder in PBS buffer and mix with vortex.

steps.title: Prewash the C
steps.instruction: Prewash the C. vulgaris suspension by incubating it in an orbital shaker at 37ºC, 150 rpm for 30 minutes. Centrifuge the suspension at 4000 g for 30 minutes. Discard the supernatant and resuspend the pellet in new PBS buffer (20 mg/mL) and mix with vortex.

steps.title: In a 24-well plate, add 1 mL of C
steps.instruction: In a 24-well plate, add 1 mL of C. vulgaris suspension to each well. Then, add the respective enzyme to each well to a final concentration of 20 µg/mL. In the corresponding control wells, add 50 µL of PBS buffer instead of the enzyme. The incubations should be performed in triplicate.

steps.title: Seal the plate and incubate in...
steps.instruction: Seal the plate and incubate in an orbital shaker at 37ºC, 60 rpm, overnight.

steps.title: Then, centrifuge the plate at 4000...
steps.instruction: Then, centrifuge the plate at 4000 g for 15 minutes and recovery the supernatant to an eppendorf.

steps.title: Boil the samples for 5 minutes...
steps.instruction: Boil the samples for 5 minutes and centrifuge at 10000 g for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Recovery the supernatant for a new...
steps.instruction: Recovery the supernatant for a new eppendorf and proceed to the determination of reducing sugars release.

steps.title: Add to a new eppendorf 600...
steps.instruction: Add to a new eppendorf 600 µL of the supernatant from the stage 7. Add to the supernatant 600 µL of DNSA reagent previously prepared ( Note 1 ).

steps.title: Note 1
steps.instruction: Note 1: DNSA reagent – 1% 3,5-Dinitrosalicylic acid; 0.2% phenol and 1% sodium hydroxide. Before use, add 200 µL of sodium sulphite (5%) and 2 µL of glucose (20%) per 20 mL of DNSA reagen (Miller, 1959).

steps.title: Mix vigorously and incubate in boiling...
steps.instruction: Mix vigorously and incubate in boiling water at 100ºC for 15 minutes.

steps.title: Place the eppendorfs on ice for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Place the eppendorfs on ice for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Measure the absorbance of samples (λ=570 nm)
steps.instruction: Measure the absorbance of samples (λ=570 nm).

steps.title: The amount of reducing sugars released...
steps.instruction: The amount of reducing sugars released is calculated using a calibration curve with sequential dilutions of a glucose solution with an initial concentration of 1 mg/mL and absorbance determined through the DNSA method (Miller, 1959).

steps.title: Repeat stages 1 to 7
steps.instruction: Repeat stages 1 to 7. At stage 3, add the enzyme mix instead of individual enzymes. The final concentration of each enzyme in the mix is 20 µg/mL in a ratio of 1:1:1:1.

steps.title: Proteolysis resistance – Place 200 µL...
steps.instruction: Proteolysis resistance – Place 200 µL of each enzyme that compose the mix, at a concentration of 1 g/L in individual eppendorfs. For each enzyme, there is a control and a treatment: 200 µL of PBS are added for the control; 200 µL of porcine pancreatin at 5 g/L are added for the treated sample. Incubate the reactions at 37 °C during regular 15 min intervals until 2 hours. Remove the samples and perform 14% SDS-PAGE gels to analyse results.

steps.title: Thermostability analysis – Incubate each enzyme...
steps.instruction: Thermostability analysis – Incubate each enzyme of the mix in a defined final concentration at the following temperatures: 30 °C, 37 °C and 40 °C to 80 °C at 5 °C intervals for 30 minutes and perform a control (no incubation). Then, cool the incubation on ice for 10 min and centrifuged at 16,100 g for 8 min at 4 °C. Recover the supernatant and quantify the protein amount in triplicate using a NanoDrop. To validate the results, analyse the supernatants by performing 14% SDS-PAGE gels.

steps.title: Repeat stages 8 to 12
steps.instruction: Repeat stages 8 to 12.

steps.title: 1 mL of the supernatants is lyophilized for 24 hours
steps.instruction: 1 mL of the supernatants is lyophilized for 24 hours.

steps.title: It should be applied the method of Folch et al
steps.instruction: It should be applied the method of Folch et al. (1957) by replacing chloroform:methanol (2:1, v/v) by dichloromethane:methanol (2:1, v/v), according to Carlson (1985) to perform the lipid extraction from supernatants.

steps.title: Perform the esterification of fatty acids...
steps.instruction: Perform the esterification of fatty acids to fatty acids methyl esters (FAME) using acid catalysis with acetylchloride-methanol solution at 80 °C for 60 min, as described by Batista et al. (2013).

steps.title: Proceed to the analysis of FAME...
steps.instruction: Proceed to the analysis of FAME as described by Coelho et al. (2019).

steps.title: The quantification of oligosaccharides in the...
steps.instruction: The quantification of oligosaccharides in the supernatants is performed by HPLC. 10 µL of supernatants are injected directly into the HPLC equipment.

steps.title: The analysis of the oligosaccharides profile...
steps.instruction: The analysis of the oligosaccharides profile is made using a Dionex CarboPac PA10 column and electrochemical detector (ECD), according to the manufacturer's instruction method (Thermo Scientific. Dionex CarboPac PA10 in Column Product Manual).

steps.title: The quantification of total oligosaccharides in...
steps.instruction: The quantification of total oligosaccharides in the supernatant is based on a standard curve, using a range of concentrations from 0.025 mM to 0.2 mM of glucose.

steps.title: The determination of protein content is...
steps.instruction: The determination of protein content is carried out by applying the classic Kjeldahl method (AOAC, 2000).

steps.title: The determination of pigments with antioxidant...
steps.instruction: The determination of pigments with antioxidant function (as chlorophylls a and b, andcarotenoids) is achieved by following Hynstova et al.(2018) protocol.

steps.title: After the plate centrifugation (stage 5),...
steps.instruction: After the plate centrifugation (stage 5), the microalgae residue is recovered.

steps.title: Lyophilize 10 mg of residues for 24 hours
steps.instruction: Lyophilize 10 mg of residues for 24 hours.

steps.title: Repeat stages 18 to 20
steps.instruction: Repeat stages 18 to 20.

steps.title: Resuspend 10 mg of residue in...
steps.instruction: Resuspend 10 mg of residue in 500 µL of PBS buffer. Add another 2 mL of PBS buffer and homogenise properly by performing “up and down” movements with the micropipette.

steps.title: Add 20 µL of the homogenate to a Neubauer chamber
steps.instruction: Add 20 µL of the homogenate to a Neubauer chamber.

steps.title: Place the chamber in an optical...
steps.instruction: Place the chamber in an optical microscope and perform cell counting.

steps.title: Add 10 µL of the homogenate...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of the homogenate prepared at stage 25 to a microscope slide.

steps.title: Then, add 2 µL of Calcofluor...
steps.instruction: Then, add 2 µL of Calcofluor White stain and 2 µL of potassium hydroxide (10%) to the sample.

steps.title: Cover with the coverslip and wait 1 minute
steps.instruction: Cover with the coverslip and wait 1 minute.

steps.title: Observe in the fluorescence microscope at...
steps.instruction: Observe in the fluorescence microscope at magnification of 400× using the DAPI filter.

steps.title: Perform the quantification of fluorescence intensity...
steps.instruction: Perform the quantification of fluorescence intensity with the Image J software.
